reasoning: Trachoma is caused by Chlamydia trachomatis, for which there is currently no approved vaccine. The leading candidate, CTH522, recently completed a Phase 1 clinical trial (published April 2024) and is nowhere near regulatory approval or wide availability. Developing and approving a vaccine typically takes 5–10 years after Phase 1. It is virtually impossible for a Trachoma vaccine to be fully developed, approved, and listed as 'Yes' on Wikipedia's 'List of infectious diseases' by February 2026. The only small chance of this resolving to 'Yes' is due to Wikipedia vandalism or a premature, non-standard edit by a user misinterpreting early-phase clinical trial results as a fully 'developed' or 'created' vaccine. To account for increasing uncertainty over time, probabilities are set to 2% for November 2025, 3% for December 2025, and 4% for February 2026.
